info.title: AIESS Energy Agent Tools
info.description: Tools for the AIESS Energy Core agent to manage battery energy storage systems, schedules, energy data, and site configuration.

get.description: Retrieves the full site configuration from DynamoDB including description, battery specs, PV arrays, grid limits, power limits, tariff, load profile, and location.
Retrieves all current schedule rules from the IoT Named Shadow including priorities P4 through P9, system mode, and safety SoC limits.

post.description: Creates or updates a schedule rule. Auto-tags source as AI. Priority must be 4 to 9. The rule_json must be a JSON string with the optimized rule format containing id, a (action with t type pw power soc target maxp max_power maxg max_grid ming min_grid str strategy pid), c (conditions with ts te sm sx gpo gpv gpx), d (days), vf vu (validity timestamps). Requires user confirmation before execution.

rule_json.description: JSON string of the rule object.

post.description: Deletes a schedule rule by its rule ID and priority level. Requires user confirmation before execution.

rule_id.description: The unique identifier of the rule to delete.

post.description: Sets the system operating mode. Valid values are automatic, semi-automatic, or manual. Requires user confirmation before execution.

mode.description: The operating mode. Must be one of: automatic, semi-automatic, manual.

post.description: Sets the battery safety SoC limits at the hardware level (P11). soc_min should be between 1 and 50 percent, soc_max should be between 50 and 100 percent. Requires user confirmation before execution.

soc_min.description: Minimum battery SoC percentage between 1 and 50.

soc_max.description: Maximum battery SoC percentage between 50 and 100.

post.description: Partially updates the site configuration in DynamoDB. Accepts a JSON string patch that will be deep-merged with existing config. Requires user confirmation before execution.

config_json.description: JSON string of partial site config to merge.

get.description: Retrieves real-time battery status including SoC, PCS power, grid power, PV power, load, and active rule from the last 2 minutes of telemetry data.
Retrieves aggregated energy statistics (averages of SoC, PCS power, grid power, PV power, load) for a specified time period. Automatically selects the optimal data bucket.

parameters.name: hours
parameters.description: Number of hours to look back. Defaults to 24 if not provided.

get.description: Retrieves TGE (Polish energy exchange) electricity prices. When hours is 0 or not provided, returns only the latest spot price. When hours is provided, returns both the current price and hourly price history for that period.

parameters.name: hours
parameters.description: Number of hours of price history. Omit or set to 0 for latest price only.

price_pln_mwh.description: Current price in PLN per MWh.

get.description: Retrieves rule history data. Returns both config snapshots (what rules were configured) and active rule execution history (which rule was actually running on firmware). Use type parameter to get only config, only active, or both.

parameters.name: hours
parameters.description: Number of hours to look back. Defaults to 24 if not provided.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of history: config for config snapshots, active for execution history, both for all. Defaults to both.

get.description: Retrieves PV production and factory load forecasts for the next hours. Returns a summary with peak PV, total PV kWh, average and peak load, plus a chart with hourly PV and load forecast curves. Use this when the user asks about future energy production, PV forecast, tomorrow's production, or expected load.

parameters.name: hours
parameters.description: Number of hours to forecast. Defaults to 48.

200.description: Forecast data with PV and load predictions.

summary.description: Forecast summary with pv_peak_kw, pv_total_kwh, load_avg_kw, load_peak_kw.
